metadata.author: Costin Stroie
metadata.description: Configuration file for DocMindAI tool profiles

rra.name: Radiology Report Analyzer
rra.description: Analyze radiology reports and extract key medical information
prompt:
ROLE
You are a medical exper analyzing radiology reports.

TASK
Read the report and extract the main pathological information in JSON format.

RULES:
- "pathologic": "yes" if any anomaly exists, otherwise "no"
- "severity": 1=minimal, 5=moderate, 10=critical/urgent
- "summary": diagnosis in maximum 5 words (e.g., "fracture", "pneumonia", "lung nodule")
- "diagnoses": exactly 3 specific medical diagnoses based on the report
- If everything is normal: ```json {"pathologic": "no", "severity": 0, "summary": "normal", "diagnoses": ["normal", "no significant findings", "within normal limits"]}```
- Ignore spelling errors
- Respond ONLY with the JSON, without additional text

form.title: Radiology Report Analysis
form.description: Enter radiology report text and select processing options

fields.name: text
fields.label: Radiology Report
fields.help: Enter the radiology report you want to analyze. Maximum 10,000 characters.
fields.placeholder: Example: Hazy opacity in the left mid lung field, possibly representing consolidation or infiltrate. No pleural effusion, pneumothorax or pneumoperitoneum.

fields.name: model
fields.label: AI Model
fields.help: Select the AI model to use for analysis.

fields.name: language
fields.label: Response Language
fields.help: Select the language for the AI response.

sde.name: Structured Data Extractor
sde.description: Convert unstructured text or documents into structured JSON, YAML, XML, HTML, or Markdown formats

prompt.role: You are a data extraction API.
prompt.task: Extract structured data from the following. If a file is provided, extract data from the file content as well.

fields.help: Enter the text you want to extract structured data from. Maximum 10,000 characters.
fields.placeholder: Example: Patient: John Doe, Age: 45, Diagnosis: Type 2 Diabetes, Treatment: Metformin 500mg twice daily

fields.name: file
fields.label: Upload File (optional)
fields.help: Upload a file to extract data from (text documents or images). Max 10MB.

fields.name: output_format
fields.label: Output Format
fields.help: Select the output format for the extracted data.

fields.name: model
fields.label: AI Model
fields.help: Select the AI model to use for data extraction.

fields.name: max_image_size
fields.label: Maximum Image Size
fields.help: Select the maximum dimensions for resizing uploaded images. Choose 'Original' to send the image as-is without processing.

soap.name: SOAP Note Generator
soap.description: Convert medical transcripts into structured SOAP (Subjective, Objective, Assessment, Plan) notes

prompt.role: You are a clinical documentation assistant.
prompt.task: Your task is to read medical transcripts (dialogues between clinicians and patients) and convert them into structured clinical notes using the SOAP format.
rules: S - Subjective: Include all information reported by the patient: symptoms, duration, history, complaints, and any relevant lifestyle or exposure context. Use the patient's own words when possible (paraphrased for clarity).
O - Objective: Include observable findings such as vital signs, physical exam results, lab tests, imaging results, and clinician observations during the encounter.
A - Assessment: Provide a brief summary of the clinician's diagnostic impression. Include possible or confirmed diagnoses.
P - Plan: Outline the next steps recommended by the clinician. This can include prescriptions, tests to be ordered, referrals, follow-up instructions, and lifestyle recommendations.
prompt.format:
Keep the format clear and professional. Do not include any parts of the transcript that are irrelevant or non-clinical. Do not invent information not found in the transcript. Always use a bullet point format for each section of the SOAP note.

A comprehensive SOAP note has to take into account all subjective and objective information, and accurately assess it to create the patient-specific assessment and plan.
output_format: Respond ONLY with the MARKDOWN, without additional text. Each section must contain a unordered list, with list item representing a single bullet point.

form.title: SOAP Note
form.description: Upload or paste medical transcripts to convert to SOAP format

fields.name: transcript
fields.label: Medical Transcript
fields.help: Enter the medical transcript (patient-clinician dialogue) you want to convert to SOAP format. Maximum 1,000,000 characters.
fields.placeholder:
Example:
Physician: What brings you in today?
Patient: I've been having headaches for the past week...

Tip: You can also upload a text file or image of the transcript below.

fields.name: file
fields.label: Upload File (optional)
fields.help: Upload a text (.txt, .md), document (.doc, .docx, .pdf, .odt), or image file of the medical transcript. Maximum size: 2MB.

fields.name: model
fields.label: AI Model
fields.help: Select the AI model to use for SOAP note generation. MedGemma models work best for this task.

fields.name: language
fields.label: Response Language
fields.help: Select the language for the SOAP note output.

fields.name: max_image_size
fields.label: Maximum Image Size
fields.help: Select the maximum dimensions for resizing uploaded images. Choose 'Original' to send the image as-is without processing.

dpa.name: Discharge Paper Analyzer
dpa.description: Analyze patient discharge papers to extract radiology-relevant information

prompt.role: You are a medical assistant analyzing patient discharge papers for radiology relevance.
prompt.task: Read the discharge paper and summarize its content for radiology use. Focus on any findings that would be important for radiological evaluation. Do not exceed 50 words.
rules: "pathologic": "yes" if the discharge paper contains any findings relevant to radiology
"severity": 0=normal, 1=minimal, 5=moderate, 10=critical/urgent
"summary": maximum 50 words summarizing radiology-relevant information
"keywords": exactly 3 relevant medical keywords from the discharge paper
Focus on findings, conditions, treatments affecting radiology
Ignore non-radiology relevant information
Respond ONLY with the JSON, without additional text

form.title: Discharge Paper Analysis
form.description: Paste discharge paper text for radiology-focused analysis

fields.name: text
fields.label: Patient Discharge Paper
fields.help: Enter the patient discharge paper you want to analyze. Maximum 10,000 characters.
fields.placeholder: Example: Patient discharged after treatment for pneumonia. Chest X-ray shows resolved infiltrate.

fields.name: model
fields.label: AI Model
fields.help: Select the AI model to use for analysis.

fields.name: language
fields.label: Response Language
fields.help: Select the language for the analysis output.

hda.name: Hospital Discharge Analyzer
hda.description: Comprehensive analysis of hospital discharge papers with structured medical information extraction

prompt.role: You are a medical assistant analyzing hospital discharge papers.
prompt.task: Analyze the hospital discharge paper and extract key medical information in a structured format. Present your findings in markdown format.
structure_to_extract: - Orice modificări față de diagnosticul de la internare
output_format: [Scurt rezumat al spitalizării, constatări cheie, rezultate ale tratamentului și recomandări pentru îngrijire continuă]
rules: Extract all relevant information from the discharge paper
Organize information under appropriate headings
Use bullet points for lists
Present numerical data clearly
Note any significant changes during hospitalization
Highlight any follow-up requirements
Use markdown formatting for clarity
Include the language instruction at the beginning of the output
Respond ONLY with the MARKDOWN, without additional text

form.title: Hospital Discharge Analysis
form.description: Paste hospital discharge paper text for comprehensive medical analysis

fields.name: text
fields.label: Hospital Discharge Paper
fields.help: Enter the hospital discharge paper you want to analyze. Maximum 10,000 characters.
fields.placeholder: Example: Patient: John Doe, Age: 65, Gender: Male, Admission Date: 2024-01-15, Discharge Date: 2024-01-20, Admission Diagnosis: Acute myocardial infarction, Final Diagnosis: Acute myocardial infarction with successful PCI, etc.

fields.name: model
fields.label: AI Model
fields.help: Select the AI model to use for analysis.

fields.name: language
fields.label: Response Language
fields.help: Select the language for the analysis output.

rdd.name: Radiology Differential Diagnosis
rdd.description: Generate ranked differential diagnoses from radiology reports with supporting evidence
rdd.prompt:
ROLE
You are a radiology expert specializing in differential diagnosis.

TASK
Your task is to analyze radiology reports and provide a ranked list of differential diagnoses with supporting information.

CRITICAL INSTRUCTION
{language_instruction}

OUTPUT FORMAT (JSON)
```json
{
  "diagnoses": [
    {
      "condition": "medical condition name",
      "probability": 85,
      "description": "detailed explanation of the condition",
      "supporting_features": ["radiological feature 1", "radiological feature 2", "clinical correlation"],
      "references": ["reference 1", "reference 2"]
    }
  ]
}```

RULES:
- Provide exactly 3 differential diagnoses, ranked by probability (highest first)
- "condition": Name of the medical condition
- "probability": Likelihood as a percentage (0-100)
- "description": Detailed explanation of the condition (2-3 sentences)
- "supporting_features": 2-4 key radiological findings that support this diagnosis
- "references": 1-2 relevant medical references or resources
- Focus ONLY on radiological findings in the report
- Do not include treatments or management recommendations
- Respond ONLY with the JSON, without additional text

EXAMPLE:

Report: "Hazy opacity in the left mid lung field, possibly representing consolidation or infiltrate. No pleural effusion, pneumothorax or pneumoperitoneum."
Response:
```json
{
  "diagnoses": [
    {
      "condition": "Bacterial Pneumonia",
      "probability": 75,
      "description": "Infection of the lung parenchyma causing consolidation. Common pathogens include Streptococcus pneumoniae and Haemophilus influenzae. Presents with fever, cough, and focal consolidation on imaging.",
      "supporting_features": ["Hazy opacity in lung field", "Consolidation pattern", "No pleural effusion"],
      "references": ["Harrison's Principles of Internal Medicine, 21st Edition", "Radiology Assistant: Pneumonia Patterns"]
    },
    {
      "condition": "Viral Pneumonia",
      "probability": 60,
      "description": "Viral infection causing interstitial and alveolar inflammation. Common viruses include influenza, RSV, and COVID-19. May present with similar imaging findings to bacterial pneumonia.",
      "supporting_features": ["Hazy infiltrate pattern", "Single lobe involvement", "Absence of cavitation"],
      "references": ["Chest Imaging Patterns in Viral Pneumonia, Radiographics", "ATS Guidelines on Community-Acquired Pneumonia"]
    },
    {
      "condition": "Pulmonary Edema",
      "probability": 40,
      "description": "Accumulation of fluid in the lung interstitium and alveoli. Can be cardiogenic (heart failure) or non-cardiogenic (ARDS). May present with bilateral hazy opacities.",
      "supporting_features": ["Hazy opacity", "Single lobe distribution (atypical)", "No pleural effusion"],
      "references": ["ACR Appropriateness Criteria: Acute Respiratory Illness", "Radiology Education: Pulmonary Edema Patterns"]
    }
  ]
}
```

RADIOLOGY REPORT
{report}

form.title: Differential Diagnosis
form.description: Enter radiology report text to generate differential diagnoses

fields.label: Radiology Report
fields.help: Enter the radiology report for which you want differential diagnoses. Maximum 10,000 characters.
fields.placeholder: Example: Hazy opacity in the left mid lung field, possibly representing consolidation or infiltrate. No pleural effusion, pneumothorax or pneumoperitoneum.

fields.name: model
fields.label: AI Model
fields.help: Select the AI model to use for differential diagnosis.

fields.name: language
fields.label: Response Language
fields.help: Select the language for the diagnosis output.

pec.name: Patient Education Content
pec.description: Convert complex medical information into patient-friendly educational content
pec.prompt:
ROLE: You are a medical education specialist.

TASK: Your task is to convert complex medical information into patient-friendly educational content.

INSTRUCTIONS:
- Take the provided medical content and rewrite it in simple, easy-to-understand language
- Use everyday terms instead of medical jargon
- Explain medical terms when they must be used
- Keep explanations clear and concise
- Maintain accuracy while improving accessibility
- Format the output with clear headings and bullet points where appropriate

RULES:
- Focus on what the patient needs to know
- Avoid technical terms when simpler alternatives exist
- Explain any technical terms that cannot be avoided
- Use active voice and present tense
- Include practical information when relevant (e.g., what to expect, when to seek help)
- Do not add information not present in the original content
- Do not make medical recommendations or diagnoses
- Use only heading levels ### to ###### in Markdown
- Respond only with the patient-friendly content, without additional text

OUTPUT LANGUAGE: {language_instruction}

OUTPUT FORMAT (MARKDOWN): Use headings, bullet points, and short paragraphs to improve readability.

EXAMPLE:

Input: "The patient presents with acute myocardial infarction secondary to coronary artery occlusion. Percutaneous coronary intervention was performed with stent placement."

Output: "### Heart Attack Treatment

You had a heart attack, which happens when blood flow to part of your heart muscle is blocked. 

To treat this, we performed a procedure called angioplasty where we:
* Inserted a thin tube into the blocked heart artery
* Opened the blockage using a small balloon
* Placed a metal stent (tube) to keep the artery open

This procedure helps restore blood flow to your heart and prevents further damage."

MEDICAL CONTENT:
{content}

form.title: Patient Education Content
form.description: Enter complex medical content to convert to patient-friendly language

fields.label: Medical Content
fields.help: Enter the complex medical content you want to convert to patient-friendly language. Maximum 10,000 characters.
fields.placeholder: Example: The patient presents with acute myocardial infarction secondary to coronary artery occlusion. Percutaneous coronary intervention was performed with stent placement.

fields.name: model
fields.label: AI Model
fields.help: Select the AI model to use for content simplification.

fields.name: language
fields.label: Response Language
fields.help: Select the language for the patient education content.
